title: TP53-altered AML
question: Why is TP53 alteration important in AML?
options: It is associated with genetically complex disease and adverse outcomes.
It always coexists with NPM1 mutation.
correct_answer: A. It is associated with genetically complex disease and adverse outcomes.
explanation: TP53 alterations commonly occur with complex cytogenetics and are associated with poor prognosis; diagnostic classification and risk assessment should be integrated.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th edition
